x-enumDescriptions.description: On-demand instant payments – non-recurring, pre-paid, variable amount, variable frequency.
Pay after use, non-recurring post-paid, variable amount, irregular frequency.
Pay upfront fixed or variable amount on a fixed date before the goods/service is delivered.
Pay on a fixed date based on usage or consumption after the goods/service is delivered.
Charge payer when the set amount is reached or monthly billing cycle, whichever comes first, before the goods/service is delivered.
Charge payer when the set amount is reached or monthly billing cycle, whichever comes first, after the goods/service is delivered.

Merchant-managed installment plan when the amount to be paid and the billing frequency are fixed, but there is a defined number of payments with the payment due after the goods/services are delivered.
Unscheduled card on file plan where the merchant can bill buyer upfront based on an agreed logic, but amount due and frequency can vary. Inclusive of automatic reload plans.
Subscription plan where the amount due and the billing frequency are fixed, and there is no defined duration with the payment due before the good/service is delivered.
Subscription plan where the amount due and the billing frequency are fixed, and there is no defined duration with the payment due after the goods/services are delivered.

prefer.name: Prefer
prefer.description:
The preferred server response upon successful completion of the request. Value is:
  • return=minimal. The server returns a minimal response to optimize communication between the API caller and the server. A minimal response includes the id, status and HATEOAS links.
  • return=representation. The server returns a complete resource representation, including the current state of the resource.

paypal_request_id.name: PayPal-Request-Id
paypal_request_id.description: The server stores keys for 72 hours.

product_id.name: product_id
product_id.description: Filters the response by a Product ID.

page_size.name: page_size
page_size.description: The number of items to return in the response.

page.name: page
page.description: A non-zero integer which is the start index of the entire list of items to return in the response. The combination of `page=1` and `page_size=20` returns the first 20 items. The combination of `page=2` and `page_size=20` returns the next 20 items.

total_required.name: total_required
total_required.description: Indicates whether to show the total count in the response.

id.name: id
id.description: The ID of the subscription.

fields.name: fields
fields.description: List of fields that are to be returned in the response. Possible value for fields are last_failed_payment and plan.

start_time.name: start_time
start_time.description: The start time of the range of transactions to list.

end_time.name: end_time
end_time.description: The end time of the range of transactions to list.

catalogs_products_v1_prefer.name: Prefer
catalogs_products_v1_prefer.description:
The preferred server response upon successful completion of the request. Value is:
  • return=minimal. The server returns a minimal response to optimize communication between the API caller and the server. A minimal response includes the id, status and HATEOAS links.
  • return=representation. The server returns a complete resource representation, including the current state of the resource.

catalogs_products_v1_paypal_request_id.name: PayPal-Request-Id
catalogs_products_v1_paypal_request_id.description: The server stores keys for 72 hours.

catalogs_products_v1_page_size.name: page_size
catalogs_products_v1_page_size.description: The number of items to return in the response.

catalogs_products_v1_page.name: page
catalogs_products_v1_page.description: A non-zero integer which is the start index of the entire list of items that are returned in the response. So, the combination of `page=1` and `page_size=20` returns the first 20 items. The combination of `page=2` and `page_size=20` returns the next 20 items.

catalogs_products_v1_total_required.name: total_required
catalogs_products_v1_total_required.description: Indicates whether to show the total items and total pages in the response.

checkout_orders_v2_paypal_request_id.name: PayPal-Request-Id
checkout_orders_v2_paypal_request_id.description: The server stores keys for 6 hours. The API callers can request the times to up to 72 hours by speaking to their Account Manager. It is mandatory for all single-step create order calls (E.g. Create Order Request with payment source information like Card, PayPal.vault_id, PayPal.billing_agreement_id, etc).

checkout_orders_v2_prefer.name: Prefer
checkout_orders_v2_prefer.description:
The preferred server response upon successful completion of the request. Value is:
  • return=minimal. The server returns a minimal response to optimize communication between the API caller and the server. A minimal response includes the id, status and HATEOAS links.
  • return=representation. The server returns a complete resource representation, including the current state of the resource.

paypal_auth_assertion.name: PayPal-Auth-Assertion
paypal_auth_assertion.description: An API-caller-provided JSON Web Token (JWT) assertion that identifies the merchant. For details, see PayPal-Auth-Assertion.

checkout_orders_v2_id.name: id
checkout_orders_v2_id.description: The ID of the order that the tracking information is associated with.

checkout_orders_v2_fields.name: fields
checkout_orders_v2_fields.description: A comma-separated list of fields that should be returned for the order. Valid filter field is `payment_source`.

customer_disputes_v1_start_time.name: start_time
customer_disputes_v1_start_time.description:
Filters the disputes in the response by a creation date and time. The start time must be within the last 180 days. Value is in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). For example, *`yyyy`*-*`MM`*-*`dd`*`T`*`HH`*:*`mm`*:*`ss`*.*`SSS`*`Z`.

You can specify either but not both the `start_time` and `disputed_transaction_id` query parameters.

disputed_transaction_id.name: disputed_transaction_id
disputed_transaction_id.description:
Filters the disputes in the response by a transaction, by ID.

You can specify either but not both the `start_time` and `disputed_transaction_id` query parameter.

customer_disputes_v1_page_size.name: page_size
customer_disputes_v1_page_size.description: Limits the number of disputes in the response to this value.

next_page_token.name: next_page_token
next_page_token.description: The token that describes the next page of results to fetch. The list disputes call returns this token in the HATEOAS links in the response.

dispute_state.name: dispute_state
dispute_state.description: Filters the disputes in the response by a state. Separate multiple values with a comma (`,`). When you specify more than one dispute_state, the response lists disputes that belong to any of the specified dispute_state.

x-enumDescriptions.description: Filters the disputes in the response to those with the REQUIRED_ACTION dispute state.
Filters the disputes in the response to those with the REQUIRED_OTHER_PARTY_ACTION dispute state.
Filters the disputes in the response to those with the UNDER_PAYPAL_REVIEW dispute state.
Filters the disputes in the response to those with the RESOLVED dispute state.
Filters the disputes in the response to those with the OPEN_INQUIRIES dispute state.
Filters the disputes in the response to those with the APPEALABLE dispute state.

update_time_before.name: update_time_before
update_time_before.description: The date and time when the dispute was last updated, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). For example, *`yyyy`*-*`MM`*-*`dd`*`T`*`HH`*:*`mm`*:*`ss`*.*`SSS`*`Z`. update_time_before must be within the last 180 days and the default is the current time.

update_time_after.name: update_time_after
update_time_after.description: The date and time when the dispute was last updated, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). For example, *`yyyy`*-*`MM`*-*`dd`*`T`*`HH`*:*`mm`*:*`ss`*.*`SSS`*`Z`. update_time_after must be within the last 180 days and the default is the maximum time (180 days) supported.

customer_disputes_v1_id.name: id
customer_disputes_v1_id.description: The ID of the dispute for which to provide the supporting information.

partner_referral_id.name: partner_referral_id
partner_referral_id.description: The ID of the partner-referrals data for which to show details.

invoicing_v2_page.name: page
invoicing_v2_page.description: The page number to be retrieved, for the list of templates. So, a combination of `page=1` and `page_size=20` returns the first 20 templates. A combination of `page=2` and `page_size=20` returns the next 20 templates.

invoicing_v2_page_size.name: page_size
invoicing_v2_page_size.description: The maximum number of templates to return in the response.

invoicing_v2_total_required.name: total_required
invoicing_v2_total_required.description: Indicates whether the to show total_pages and total_items in the response.

invoicing_v2_fields.name: fields
invoicing_v2_fields.description: The fields to return in the response. Value is `all` or `none`. To return only the template name, ID, and default attributes, specify `none`.

invoice_id.name: invoice_id
invoice_id.description: The ID of the draft invoice to delete.

transaction_id.name: transaction_id
transaction_id.description: The ID of the external refund transaction to delete.

send_to_recipient.name: send_to_recipient
send_to_recipient.description: Indicates whether to send the invoice update notification to the recipient.

send_to_invoicer.name: send_to_invoicer
send_to_invoicer.description: Indicates whether to send the invoice update notification to the merchant.

template_id.name: template_id
template_id.description: The ID of the template to delete.

invoicing_v2_id.name: id
invoicing_v2_id.description: The invoice id of the account.

anchor_type.name: anchor_type
anchor_type.description: Filters the webhooks in the response by an `anchor_id` entity type.

webhook_id.name: webhook_id
webhook_id.description: The ID of the webhook for which to list subscriptions.

webhook_lookup_id.name: webhook_lookup_id
webhook_lookup_id.description: The ID of the webhook lookup to delete.

notifications_webhooks_v1_page_size.name: page_size
notifications_webhooks_v1_page_size.description: The number of webhook event notifications to return in the response.

notifications_webhooks_v1_start_time.name: start_time
notifications_webhooks_v1_start_time.description: Filters the webhook event notifications in the response to those created on or after this date and time and on or before the `end_time` value. Both values are in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6) format. Example: `start_time=2013-03-06T11:00:00Z`.

notifications_webhooks_v1_end_time.name: end_time
notifications_webhooks_v1_end_time.description: Filters the webhook event notifications in the response to those created on or after the `start_time` and on or before this date and time. Both values are in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6) format. Example: `end_time=2013-03-06T11:00:00Z`.

notifications_webhooks_v1_transaction_id.name: transaction_id
notifications_webhooks_v1_transaction_id.description: Filters the response to a single transaction, by ID.

event_type.name: event_type
event_type.description: Filters the response to a single event.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description: The ID of the webhook event notification to resend.

payment_experience_web_experience_profil.name: PayPal-Request-Id
payment_experience_web_experience_profil.description: The server stores keys for three hours.

payment_experience_web_experience_profil.name: id
payment_experience_web_experience_profil.description: The ID of the profile to delete.

authorization_id.name: authorization_id
authorization_id.description: The PayPal-generated ID for the authorized payment to void.

payments_payment_v2_paypal_auth_assertio.name: PayPal-Auth-Assertion
payments_payment_v2_paypal_auth_assertio.description:
An API-caller-provided JSON Web Token (JWT) assertion that identifies the merchant. For details, see [PayPal-Auth-Assertion](/docs/api/reference/api-requests/#paypal-auth-assertion).
Note:For three party transactions in which a partner is managing the API calls on behalf of a merchant, the partner must identify the merchant using either a PayPal-Auth-Assertion header or an access token with target_subject.

payments_payment_v2_paypal_request_id.name: PayPal-Request-Id
payments_payment_v2_paypal_request_id.description: The server stores keys for 45 days.

payments_payment_v2_prefer.name: Prefer
payments_payment_v2_prefer.description:
The preferred server response upon successful completion of the request. Value is:
  • return=minimal. The server returns a minimal response to optimize communication between the API caller and the server. A minimal response includes the id, status and HATEOAS links.
  • return=representation. The server returns a complete resource representation, including the current state of the resource.

capture_id.name: capture_id
capture_id.description: The PayPal-generated ID for the captured payment to refund.

refund_id.name: refund_id
refund_id.description: The PayPal-generated ID for the refund for which to show details.

payments_payouts_batch_v1_paypal_request.name: PayPal-Request-Id
payments_payouts_batch_v1_paypal_request.description: The server stores keys for 30 days.

payments_payouts_batch_v1_id.name: id
payments_payouts_batch_v1_id.description: The ID of the payout for which to show details.

payments_payouts_batch_v1_fields.name: fields
payments_payouts_batch_v1_fields.description: Shows details for only the specified fields.

payments_payouts_batch_v1_page.name: page
payments_payouts_batch_v1_page.description: A non-zero integer representing the page of the results.

payments_payouts_batch_v1_page_size.name: page_size
payments_payouts_batch_v1_page_size.description: The maximum number of results to return at one time. Value is a non-negative, non-zero integer. If the user chooses pagination, the maximum page is `1000`.

payments_payouts_batch_v1_total_required.name: total_required
payments_payouts_batch_v1_total_required.description: Indicates whether to show the total items and total pages count in the response.

payout_item_id.name: payout_item_id
payout_item_id.description: The ID of the payout item to cancel.

reporting_transactions_v1_transaction_id.name: transaction_id
reporting_transactions_v1_transaction_id.description:
Filters the transactions in the response by a PayPal transaction ID. A valid transaction ID is 17 characters long, except for an order ID, which is 19 characters long.
Note: A transaction ID is not unique in the reporting system. The response can list two transactions with the same ID. One transaction can be balance affecting while the other is non-balance affecting.

transaction_type.name: transaction_type
transaction_type.description: Filters the transactions in the response by a PayPal transaction event code. See [Transaction event codes](/docs/integration/direct/transaction-search/transaction-event-codes/).

transaction_status.name: transaction_status
transaction_status.description:
Filters the transactions in the response by a PayPal transaction status code. Value is:
Status codeDescription
DPayPal or merchant rules denied the transaction.
PThe transaction is pending. The transaction was created but waits for another payment process to complete, such as an ACH transaction, before the status changes to S.
SThe transaction successfully completed without a denial and after any pending statuses.
VA successful transaction was reversed and funds were refunded to the original sender.

transaction_amount.name: transaction_amount
transaction_amount.description:
Filters the transactions in the response by a gross transaction amount range. Specify the range as ` TO `, where `` is the lower limit of the gross PayPal transaction amount and `` is the upper limit of the gross transaction amount. Specify the amounts in lower denominations. For example, to search for transactions from $5.00 to $10.05, specify `[500 TO 1005]`.
Note:The values must be URL encoded.

transaction_currency.name: transaction_currency
transaction_currency.description: Filters the transactions in the response by a [three-character ISO-4217 currency code](/api/rest/reference/currency-codes/) for the PayPal transaction currency.

start_date.name: start_date
start_date.description: Filters the transactions in the response by a start date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Seconds are required. Fractional seconds are optional.

end_date.name: end_date
end_date.description: Filters the transactions in the response by an end date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Seconds are required. Fractional seconds are optional. The maximum supported range is 31 days.

payment_instrument_type.name: payment_instrument_type
payment_instrument_type.description:
Filters the transactions in the response by a payment instrument type. Value is either:
  • CREDITCARD. Returns a direct credit card transaction with a corresponding value.
  • DEBITCARD. Returns a debit card transaction with a corresponding value.
If you omit this parameter, the API does not apply this filter.

store_id.name: store_id
store_id.description: Filters the transactions in the response by a store ID.

terminal_id.name: terminal_id
terminal_id.description: Filters the transactions in the response by a terminal ID.

reporting_transactions_v1_fields.name: fields
reporting_transactions_v1_fields.description:
Indicates which fields appear in the response. Value is a single field or a comma-separated list of fields. The transaction_info value returns only the transaction details in the response. To include all fields in the response, specify fields=all. Valid fields are:
  • transaction_info. The transaction information. Includes the ID of the PayPal account of the payee, the PayPal-generated transaction ID, the PayPal-generated base ID, the PayPal reference ID type, the transaction event code, the date and time when the transaction was initiated and was last updated, the transaction amounts including the PayPal fee, any discounts, insurance, the transaction status, and other information about the transaction.
  • payer_info. The payer information. Includes the PayPal customer account ID and the payer's email address, primary phone number, name, country code, address, and whether the payer is verified or unverified.
  • shipping_info. The shipping information. Includes the recipient's name, the shipping method for this order, the shipping address for this order, and the secondary address associated with this order.
  • auction_info. The auction information. Includes the name of the auction site, the auction site URL, the ID of the customer who makes the purchase in the auction, and the date and time when the auction closes.
  • cart_info. The cart information. Includes an array of item details, whether the item amount or the shipping amount already includes tax, and the ID of the invoice for PayPal-generated invoices.
  • incentive_info. An array of incentive detail objects. Each object includes the incentive, such as a special offer or coupon, the incentive amount, and the incentive program code that identifies a merchant loyalty or incentive program.
  • store_info. The store information. Includes the ID of the merchant store and the terminal ID for the checkout stand in the merchant store.

balance_affecting_records_only.name: balance_affecting_records_only
balance_affecting_records_only.description:
Indicates whether the response includes only balance-impacting transactions or all transactions. Value is either:
  • Y. The default. The response includes only balance transactions.
  • N. The response includes all transactions.

reporting_transactions_v1_page_size.name: page_size
reporting_transactions_v1_page_size.description: The number of items to return in the response. So, the combination of `page=1` and `page_size=20` returns the first 20 items. The combination of `page=2` and `page_size=20` returns the next 20 items.

reporting_transactions_v1_page.name: page
reporting_transactions_v1_page.description: The zero-relative start index of the entire list of items that are returned in the response. So, the combination of `page=1` and `page_size=20` returns the first 20 items.

as_of_time.name: as_of_time
as_of_time.description: List balances in the response at the date time provided, will return the last refreshed balance in the system when not provided.

currency_code.name: currency_code
currency_code.description: Filters the transactions in the response by a [three-character ISO-4217 currency code](/api/rest/reference/currency-codes/) for the PayPal transaction currency.

shipping_shipment_tracking_v1_transactio.name: transaction_id
shipping_shipment_tracking_v1_transactio.description: Filters the tracking information that appears in the response by a PayPal transaction ID.

tracking_number.name: tracking_number
tracking_number.description: Filters the tracking information that appears in the response by a tracking number.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.description: Encrypted PayPal Account ID of the buyer or seller.

shipping_shipment_tracking_v1_id.name: id
shipping_shipment_tracking_v1_id.description: The ID of the tracker in the transaction_id-tracking_number format.

vault_payment_tokens_v3_paypal_request_i.name: PayPal-Request-Id
vault_payment_tokens_v3_paypal_request_i.description: The server stores keys for 3 hours.

customer_id.name: customer_id
customer_id.description: A unique identifier representing a specific customer in merchant's/partner's system or records.

vault_payment_tokens_v3_page_size.name: page_size
vault_payment_tokens_v3_page_size.description: A non-negative, non-zero integer indicating the maximum number of results to return at one time.

vault_payment_tokens_v3_page.name: page
vault_payment_tokens_v3_page.description: A non-negative, non-zero integer representing the page of the results.

vault_payment_tokens_v3_total_required.name: total_required
vault_payment_tokens_v3_total_required.description: A boolean indicating total number of items (total_items) and pages (total_pages) are expected to be returned in the response.

This privilege allows client to update a dispute (customer actions).

Permission to do any real-time payment and manage-payment experience profiles with support for sale, authorize, and order intents.

tags.name: plans
tags.description: Use the `/billing/plans` resource to create and manage plans.

tags.name: subscriptions
tags.description: Use the `/billing/subscriptions` resource to create and manage subscriptions.

tags.name: subscriptions-buyer-consent
tags.description: Use the `/billing/subscriptions/approval-tokens` resource for subscriptions buyer consent flow.

tags.name: products
tags.description: Use `/products` resource to create and manage products.

tags.name: orders
tags.description: Use the `/orders` resource to create, update, retrieve, authorize, capture and track orders.

tags.name: trackers
tags.description: Use the `/trackers` resource to update and retrieve tracking information for PayPal orders.

tags.name: externalcallback
tags.description: PayPal will use the callback url defined in the create order request to send merchants shipping address and shipping options data while the buyer is in the PayPal checkout.

tags.name: disputes
tags.description: Use the `/disputes` resource to list disputes, create disputes, show dispute details, and partially a dispute. Normally, an agent at PayPal creates disputes but now you can run test cases in the sandbox that create disputes.

tags.name: disputes-actions
tags.description:
Use the `/disputes` resource with a dispute ID and an action to:
  • Accept a claim.
  • Accept an offer to resolve a dispute.
  • Acknowledge the return of an item related to a dispute.
  • Settle a dispute.
  • Appeal a dispute.
  • Cancel a dispute.
  • Change the reason for a dispute.
  • Deny an offer to resolve dispute.
  • Escalate a dispute to a claim.
  • Make an offer to resolve a dispute.
  • Make an offer to resolve a dispute.
  • Provide evidence for a dispute.
  • Provide supporting information for dispute.
  • In the sandbox, update the dispute status.
  • Send a message about a dispute to the other party in the dispute.

tags.name: referred-disputes
tags.description: Use the `/customer/referred-disputes` resource to create a dispute for a referred case that was created by a partner or marketplace, show details for a referred dispute, by ID, notify referred refunds completed to PayPal, notify PayPal about referred dispute adjudication updates, and determine the dispute eligibility for referred disputes.

tags.name: validate-eligibility
tags.description: Use the `/validate-eligibility/` resource to determine whether you can create a case for a transaction.

tags.name: compute-metrics
tags.description: Use the `/compute-metrics` resource to provide metrics for all disputes.

tags.name: validate-referred-dispute-eligibility
tags.description: Use the `/validate-referred-dispute-eligibility` resource to determine whether you can create a referred case for a transaction, by encrypted transaction ID.

tags.name: referred-disputes-actions
tags.description: Use the `/referred-disputes` resource with a dispute ID and an action to notify PayPal about adjudication updates for a referred dispute and notify PayPal about a refund for a referred dispute.

tags.name: partner-actions
tags.description: Use the `/disputes/{id}/partner-actions/` resource to show dispute action details and partially update a dispute action.

tags.name: search-suggestions
tags.description: Use the `/search-suggestions` resource to show search suggestions.

tags.name: dispute-events
tags.description: Use the `/dispute-events` resource to send dispute events.

tags.name: refund-preference
tags.description: Use the `/disputes/{id}/provide-refund-preference` resource to provide refund preferences for the dispute.

tags.name: return-compensation
tags.description: Use the `/disputes/{id}/return-compensation` resource to return the compensation on the dispute provided by PayPal to the customer.

tags.name: partner-referrals
tags.description: Enables you to create and get information about shared customer data.

tags.name: invoices
tags.description: Use the `/invoices` resource to create, update, and send invoices and invoice reminders. To manage invoices, you can also list invoices, show details for invoices, delete draft invoices, and cancel sent invoices. You can also record payments for invoices to mark them as fully or partially paid, or record refunds for invoices to mark them as fully or partially refunded. You can create QR codes for invoices that can be scanned, viewed, and paid by a mobile phone.

tags.name: search-invoices
tags.description: Use the `/search-invoices` resource to search for and list invoices that match search criteria.

tags.name: templates
tags.description:
Use the `/templates` resource to create, list, show details for, update, and delete invoice templates. Use the `/templates` resource when you create a third-party invoicing application. For instance, a business can create a template with predefined invoice data. Later, the business can select the template to populate the invoice data.
Note: To upload a logo to display on an invoice, you can use the Template Settings dashboard to create a template. When you create an invoice, you can use the URI for that logo.

tags.name: merchant-config
tags.description: Use the `/merchant-config` resource to create, update and show details of merchant configuration.

tags.name: estimates
tags.description: Use the /estimate resource to create, update, and send estimates.

tags.name: batch-tasks
tags.description: Use the /batch-tasks resource to create, delete and retrieve batch related tasks.

tags.name: stored-discounts
tags.description: Use the /stored-discounts resource to create and retrieve stored-discounts are specific to merchants

tags.name: Search Enhancement
tags.description: This section contains the list of api, used to power invoicing search based on Index Organised Table(s).

tags.name: Invoicing Multi Tenancy

tags.name: Insights
tags.description: Use the /insights resource to retrieve aggregated metrics specific to merchants.

tags.name: invoicing-product-features
tags.description: This section contains the list of invoicing-product-features related api's.

tags.name: conditional-rules
tags.description: This section contains api's related to conditional rules.

tags.name: invoice-payment-attempt
tags.description: This section contains api's related to invoice payment attempt.

tags.name: invoice-connection-details

tags.name: webhooks-events

tags.name: simulate-event
tags.description:
Use the `/simulate-event` resource to use a sample payload to simulate a webhook event. The events that this call generates only serve to validate the connection to the listener URL and to show how webhook events look.
Note: You can also use the Webhooks simulator to simulate webhook events.

tags.name: publish-event-with-payload

tags.name: publish-event-with-reference
tags.description: Use the `/publish-event-with-reference` resource to publish an event with a specified reference.

tags.name: webhook-event-delivery-status

tags.name: webhooks-errors

tags.description: Use the `/vault/setup-tokens` resource to create and retrieve temporary vault payment methods.

tags.name: tokens
tags.description: Use the `/vault/tokens` resource to detokenize a vault token to retrieve details associated to the underlying payment source.

tags.name: tokens-session
tags.description: Use the `/vault/tokens/{id}/session` resource to read the token session cache and retrieve details associated to the underlying payment source.

tags.name: customers
tags.description: Use the `/vault/customers` resource to create, update, get and delete a customer.

tags.name: payment-method-credentials
tags.description: Use the `/vault/payment-method-credentials` resource to create payment method credentials
